projects.title: Add Two Numbers
projects.description: Write a function that takes two integers and returns their sum.
projects.logicExplanation: Simple addition: return the sum of two integers.
projects.codeExplanation: The function `add()` takes two int parameters and returns their sum. In `main()`, the user inputs two numbers, calls the function, and prints the result.

projects.title: Subtract Two Numbers
projects.description: Write a function that subtracts the second integer from the first and returns the result.
projects.logicExplanation: Subtraction: return the difference of two integers.
projects.codeExplanation: The function `subtract()` takes two ints and returns the result of `a - b`. `main()` handles I/O.

projects.title: Multiply Two Numbers
projects.description: Write a function that multiplies two integers and returns the product.
projects.logicExplanation: Multiplication: return the product of two integers.
projects.codeExplanation: `multiply()` returns `a * b`. `main()` reads two numbers and prints the product.

projects.title: Divide Two Numbers (Integer Division)
projects.description: Write a function that divides the first integer by the second (non‑zero) and returns the integer quotient.
projects.logicExplanation: Integer division: return the quotient of two integers (assuming divisor non‑zero).
projects.codeExplanation: `divide()` returns `a / b`. `main()` checks for division by zero (optional) and prints the quotient.

projects.title: Factorial (Iterative)
projects.description: Write a function to compute the factorial of a non‑negative integer using iteration.
projects.logicExplanation: Factorial of n computed iteratively by multiplying from 1 to n.
projects.codeExplanation: `factorialIter()` uses a loop to accumulate product. `main()` reads n and prints result.

projects.title: Factorial (Recursive)
projects.description: Write a recursive function to compute the factorial of a non‑negative integer.
projects.logicExplanation: Factorial of n (n!) defined recursively: n! = n * (n-1)! with 0! = 1.
projects.codeExplanation: `factorialRec()` calls itself with n-1 until base case. `main()` reads n and prints result.

projects.title: Check Prime Number
projects.description: Write a function that returns 1 if a number is prime, 0 otherwise.
projects.logicExplanation: A prime number is greater than 1 and divisible only by 1 and itself. Check divisibility up to sqrt(n).
projects.codeExplanation: `isPrime()` handles numbers <2, then loops from 2 to sqrt(n). Returns 1 if prime, 0 otherwise. `main()` reads a number and prints the result.

projects.title: Check Even or Odd
projects.description: Write a function that returns 1 if a number is even, 0 if odd.
projects.logicExplanation: Standard algorithm for Check Even or Odd.
projects.codeExplanation: The C code implements Check Even or Odd as described in the example.

projects.title: Maximum of Two Numbers
projects.description: Write a function that returns the larger of two integers.
projects.logicExplanation: Compare two numbers and return the larger one.
projects.codeExplanation: `max()` returns a if a > b else b. `main()` reads two numbers and prints the maximum.

projects.title: Minimum of Two Numbers
projects.description: Write a function that returns the smaller of two integers.
projects.logicExplanation: Compare two numbers and return the smaller one.
projects.codeExplanation: `min()` returns a if a < b else b. `main()` reads two numbers and prints the minimum.

projects.title: Power (Iterative)
projects.description: Write a function to compute base raised to exponent (non‑negative) using iteration.
projects.logicExplanation: Compute base raised to exponent by repeated multiplication.
projects.codeExplanation: `powerIter()` uses a loop to multiply base exponent times. `main()` reads base and exponent and prints result.

projects.title: Power (Recursive)
projects.description: Write a recursive function to compute base raised to exponent (non‑negative).
projects.logicExplanation: Recursive power: base^exp = base * base^(exp-1) with base^0 = 1.
projects.codeExplanation: `powerRec()` calls itself with decreasing exponent. `main()` reads base and exponent and prints result.

projects.title: Sum of Digits
projects.description: Write a function that returns the sum of the digits of a positive integer.
projects.learningOutcome: Digit extraction using modulo and division.
projects.logicExplanation: Extract digits by repeatedly taking modulo 10 and dividing by 10, accumulating the sum.
projects.codeExplanation: `sumOfDigits()` uses a while loop to add last digit and remove it. `main()` reads a number and prints the sum.

projects.title: Reverse a Number
projects.description: Write a function that returns the reverse of a given integer.
projects.logicExplanation: Build the reversed number by repeatedly taking the last digit and appending it.
projects.codeExplanation: `reverseNumber()` uses a loop: rev = rev*10 + digit. `main()` reads a number and prints the reversed number.

projects.title: Palindrome Number
projects.description: Write a function that checks if a number is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards).
projects.logicExplanation: A palindrome reads the same forwards and backwards. Compare the number with its reverse.
projects.codeExplanation: `isPalindrome()` reverses the number and compares with original. `main()` reads a number and prints whether it's a palindrome.

projects.title: Fibonacci (Iterative)
projects.description: Write a function to return the nth Fibonacci number using iteration.
projects.logicExplanation: Fibonacci computed iteratively using a loop and two variables.
projects.codeExplanation: `fibIter()` uses a for loop to build the sequence up to n. `main()` reads n and prints the nth Fibonacci number.

projects.title: Fibonacci (Recursive)
projects.description: Write a recursive function to return the nth Fibonacci number.
projects.codeExplanation: `fibRec()` calls itself recursively. `main()` reads n and prints the nth Fibonacci number.

projects.title: GCD (Euclidean Algorithm)
projects.description: Write a function to compute the greatest common divisor of two integers using the Euclidean algorithm.
projects.logicExplanation: Greatest Common Divisor using Euclidean algorithm: GCD(a,b) = GCD(b, a%b).
projects.codeExplanation: `gcd()` uses recursion or loop to apply the Euclidean algorithm. `main()` reads two numbers and prints their GCD.

projects.title: LCM
projects.description: Write a function to compute the least common multiple of two integers.
projects.logicExplanation: Least Common Multiple: LCM(a,b) = (a*b) / GCD(a,b).
projects.codeExplanation: `lcm()` first computes GCD using a helper function, then applies the formula. `main()` reads two numbers and prints LCM.

projects.title: Count Digits
projects.description: Write a function that returns the number of digits in a positive integer.
projects.logicExplanation: Count the number of digits in a positive integer by repeatedly dividing by 10.
projects.codeExplanation: `countDigits()` uses a while loop to divide until zero, counting steps. `main()` reads a number and prints the digit count.

projects.title: Armstrong Number
projects.description: Write a function to check if a number is an Armstrong number (sum of its digits each raised to the power of number of digits equals the number).
projects.logicExplanation: An Armstrong number (narcissistic) equals the sum of its digits each raised to the power of the number of digits.
projects.codeExplanation: `isArmstrong()` counts digits, then computes sum of each digit raised to that power, and compares with original. `main()` reads a number and prints result.

projects.title: Perfect Number
projects.description: Write a function to check if a number is perfect (equal to sum of its proper divisors).
projects.logicExplanation: A perfect number equals the sum of its proper divisors (excluding itself).
projects.codeExplanation: `isPerfect()` loops from 1 to n/2, sums divisors, and checks if sum equals n. `main()` reads a number and prints result.

projects.title: Sum of Array Elements
projects.description: Write a function that takes an integer array and its size, and returns the sum of all elements.
projects.learningOutcome: Array traversal, function with array parameter.
projects.logicExplanation: Iterate through the array and accumulate the total.
projects.codeExplanation: `sumArray()` takes an array and its size, uses a for loop to add each element, and returns the sum. `main()` reads array and prints sum.

projects.title: Average of Array Elements
projects.description: Write a function that takes an integer array and its size, and returns the average as a float.
projects.logicExplanation: Compute the average as sum of elements divided by number of elements.
projects.codeExplanation: `averageArray()` calls sumArray() (or computes sum directly) and divides by n (using float division). `main()` reads array and prints average.

projects.title: Linear Search
projects.description: Write a function that searches for a key in an array. Return the index if found, otherwise -1.
projects.exampleText:
Enter size: 6
Enter elements: 2 4 6 8 10 12
Enter key: 8
projects.logicExplanation: Search for a key in an array by checking each element sequentially. Return index if found, else -1.
projects.codeExplanation: `linearSearch()` loops through array, compares each element with key, returns index if found. `main()` reads array and key, prints result.
